title: ODI Matrix — Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services (NAICS 518210)

sections.title: Step 1 — Error Statement Collection
sections.content:
26 needs from Step 06 (JTBD_data_processing_hosting) + 8 product-related needs from Step 10 (FIT §10a) = **34 total needs**.

Step 10 entries injected with `job_step_number=0`, `job_step_name="product_constraint"`, `impact_category="reliability"`, `product_related=true` per merge rule.

Market context: CDU secondary-loop flow sensing for GPU / HPC liquid-cooled IT (OT-3 primary, OT-2 partial). Safety-of-IT-load dominates upstream opportunity stack. No needs skipped.

---

sections.title: Step 2 — Satisfaction Recalibration (Incumbent-Grounded)
sections.content:
**Incumbent profiles used for recalibration** (from JTBD §2.4, FIT §11):

| Incumbent | Share | Mechanism | Strengths | Weaknesses |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Electromagnetic (E+H Promag H et al.)** | 35% | Faraday induction | ±0.5% accuracy, no moving parts, DN2–DN150, HART/Modbus/PROFIBUS/EtherNet-IP, 100:1 turndown, bidirectional, empty-pipe detection via conductivity | 5–10× cost, larger footprint, higher power, flanged not plug-in, requires conductive fluid |
| Ultrasonic transit-time | 30% | Acoustic A/B | (subject — excluded) | (subject — excluded) |
| Turbine / impeller | 15% | Rotational | Low cost, simple pulse | Bearing wear, pressure drop, no diagnostics, ferrous particle jamming |
| Vortex | 10% | Bluff-body shedding | No moving parts, simple | Min-Reynolds floor, vibration sensitive, poor at low flow |
| Thermal-dispersion / paddle switch | 10% | Binary threshold | Cheap redundant cutout | On/off only, no kW duty, no telemetry |

*Leading incumbent in CDU deployments is electromagnetic (E+H Promag H). Recalibration weights mag heavily with a floor set by turbine/vortex coverage in lower-tier CDU builds.*

### Recalibration Table — Step 06 Needs

| # | Statement (abbreviated) | Imp | Sat_orig | Sat_recal | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| ES-01 | Turndown covers idle→peak pump | 8 | 5 | **6** | Mag 100:1 turndown covers variable-speed; turbine 30:1 adequate |
| ES-02 | Single platform DN15–DN25 | 7 | 4 | **7** | Mag DN2–DN150 with common transmitter; Promag H full coverage |
| ES-03 | Commissioning for water/PG25/EG25 | 7 | 4 | **5** | Mag needs no fluid config; ultrasonic/vortex do. Mixed |
| ES-04 | Install orientation <2% bias | 8 | 5 | **6** | Mag orientation-insensitive; ultrasonic/vortex biased |
| ES-05 | Modbus/BACnet/MQTT BMS | 7 | 5 | **7** | Mag ships HART/Modbus/PROFIBUS/EtherNet-IP native |
| ES-06 | Empty-pipe detect <5s | 9 | 3 | **4** | Mag has conductivity-based empty-pipe; turbine/vortex cannot |
| ES-07 | Self-diag at start cycle | 7 | 4 | **5** | Mag via HART; turbine none |
| ES-08 | Gate GPU on OCP 1.5 L/min/kW | 9 | 4 | **4** | Flow reading available; OCP-specific interlock logic not provided |
| ES-09 | ±2% over 40,000 h | 9 | 5 | **6** | Mag drift-stable; turbine drifts 2–5%; mixed |
| ES-10 | Air >2% vol after glycol top-up | 8 | 4 | **5** | Mag insensitive to air; turbine jams; ultrasonic loses signal |
| ES-11 | Step-change loss-of-flow <2s | 10 | 4 | **5** | Mag response 0.1–1s adequate; turbine slower |
| ES-12 | Single-body flow×ΔT kW, sub-second | 8 | 4 | **3** | Integrated temp is rare across incumbents; Promag H optional |
| ES-13 | ΔP <50 mbar @ 30 L/min | 7 | 6 | **7** | Mag zero-drop; turbine/vortex moderate drop |
| ES-14 | Pulsation oscillation <5% | 6 | 5 | **5** | Mag has damping; ultrasonic/turbine susceptible |
| ES-15 | Reverse-flow detect <1s | 7 | 4 | **6** | Mag bidirectional; turbine/paddle cannot resolve direction |
| ES-16 | Undetected degradation >10% | 9 | 5 | **5** | Mag stable but no degradation trending; turbine drifts silently |
| ES-17 | Drift ±0.5% / 30d over 24mo | 8 | 4 | **5** | Mag stable but no continuous drift alarm logic |
| ES-18 | 1-sec telemetry to DCIM | 8 | 5 | **6** | Mag with Modbus/EtherNet-IP streams at 1 Hz |
| ES-19 | Biofilm/scale <2% / 5yr | 7 | 5 | **6** | Mag electrodes tolerate fouling; turbine blades accumulate |
| ES-20 | Incipient leak signatures | 9 | 4 | **3** | No incumbent provides sub-threshold leak pattern recognition |
| ES-21 | Acoustic-coupling drift <1% / 5yr | 7 | 5 | **5** | Ultrasonic-specific; mag unaffected but minority share |
| ES-22 | Replace without draining | 8 | 3 | **3** | Mag flanged/bulky; turbine/vortex inline — all require drain |
| ES-23 | Recal <1 per 5-yr cycle | 7 | 4 | **6** | Mag rarely needs field recal; turbine wears and drifts |
| ES-24 | Remote firmware push via BMS | 6 | 3 | **3** | Mag HART limited OTA; no incumbent offers full remote update |
| ES-25 | ASHRAE TC 9.9 traceable telemetry | 8 | 4 | **5** | Mag with ISO 17025 traceability available; turbine rare |
| ES-26 | Hyperscaler escalations <24mo | 8 | 4 | **5** | Mag reliability good but cost-driven underdeployment |

### Recalibration Table — Step 10 Product-Related Needs

| # | Statement (abbreviated) | Imp | Sat_orig | Sat_recal | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| PRN-01 | Glycol inhibitor acoustic drift | 7 | 4 | **6** | Mag insensitive to sound speed; ultrasonic-only failure mode |
| PRN-02 | 90 °C housing limit (ASHRAE W4/W5) | 5 | 5 | **7** | Metal-housed mag/Coriolis rated 130 °C+ |
| PRN-03 | DN20 4.2 L/min SNR floor vs OCP | 8 | 4 | **6** | Mag resolves sub-0.1 m/s; ultrasonic hits SNR floor |
| PRN-04 | Sub-threshold air bias 1–2% vol | 8 | 3 | **5** | Mag insensitive; turbine jams; mixed incumbent coverage |
| PRN-05 | Empty-pipe blackout during drain-and-fill | 7 | 4 | **5** | Mag conductivity empty-pipe flag; mechanical impeller still rotates in partial fill |
| PRN-06 | Single-chord path bias in compact manifold | 7 | 5 | **6** | Multi-path ultrasonic/mag are profile-insensitive |
| PRN-07 | Speed-of-sound transient >5 °C/min | 7 | 5 | **6** | Mag temp-independent by principle |
| PRN-08 | 10 bar polymer housing limit | 5 | 4 | **6** | Metal-bodied mag/Coriolis rated 16–40 bar |

---

sections.title: Step 3 — Opportunity Scores (Deterministic)
sections.content:
Formula: `opportunity_score = importance + max(importance - satisfaction, 0)`
Underserved: `importance > 7 AND satisfaction < 5`
Overserved: `importance < 3 AND satisfaction > 7`

### Step 06 Needs — Scored

| # | Statement (abbreviated) | Imp | Sat | Opp | Under? | Over? | Impact | prod_rel |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| ES-01 | Turndown covers idle→peak pump | 8 | 6 | **10.00** | no | no | reliability | false |
| ES-02 | Single platform DN15–DN25 | 7 | 7 | **7.00** | no | no | cost | true |

sections.title: Step 4 — Market ODI Summary
sections.content:
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| **Market** | Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services (NAICS 518210) |
| **Total Needs** | 34 |
| **Underserved Count** | 5 |
| **Overserved Count** | 0 |
| **Avg Opportunity Score** | 9.85 |

### Top 5 Opportunities

| Rank | # | Error Statement | Imp | Sat | Opp | Zone |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | ES-11 | Minimize the time to detect a step-change loss-of-flow event (leak, valve closure, pump trip) to under 2 seconds | 10 | 5 | **15.00** | Underserved |
| 2 | ES-20 | Increase the ability to detect incipient leak signatures (sub-threshold flow decay) before visible coolant loss | 9 | 3 | **15.00** | Underserved |
| 3 | ES-06 | Minimize the time to detect empty-pipe or air-lock in the secondary loop before IT power-on to under 5 seconds | 9 | 4 | **14.00** | Underserved |
| 4 | ES-08 | Minimize the likelihood of enabling GPU load with secondary-loop flow below the OCP 1.5 L/min/kW safety threshold | 9 | 4 | **14.00** | Underserved |
| 5 | ES-16 | Minimize the likelihood of GPU throttling events triggered by undetected flow degradation exceeding 10% from setpoint | 9 | 5 | **13.00** | Underserved |

**Runners-up at Opp=13:** ES-09 (40,000 h accuracy), ES-12 (single-body kW), ES-22 (replace without drain).

### Opportunity Landscape

| Zone | Score Range | Count | % of Total |
|---|---|---|---|
| Highly Underserved | > 15 | 0 | 0.0% |
| Underserved | 12–15 | 8 | 23.5% |
| Appropriately Served | 10–12 | 9 | 26.5% |
| Overserved | < 10 | 17 | 50.0% |

**Pattern:** Top opportunities are all **safety-of-IT-load detection** needs (loss-of-flow, empty-pipe, OCP gating, incipient leaks, degradation). This market rewards **digital anomaly detection** over raw metrological precision — uptime and thermal-runaway prevention dominate over ±0.5% accuracy.

---

sections.title: Step 5 — Cross-Check vs Feature-Market Fit
sections.content:
| Metric | Value | Source |
|---|---|---|
| ODI avg_opportunity_score | 9.85 | This analysis |
| Feature-Fit avg (tech features) | 5.73 | FIT_data_processing_hosting §09a (mean of 7 overall_fit values: 6.00, 6.56, 3.56, 6.78, 5.00, 7.56, 4.67) |

**Cross-check thresholds:**

| Condition | Triggered? | Note |
|---|---|---|
| ODI avg > 12 AND Feature-Fit avg < 4 | **NO** | Feature-Fit avg = 5.73 |
| ODI avg < 8 AND Feature-Fit avg > 7 | **NO** | ODI avg = 9.85 |

**No inconsistencies detected.** The top-5 opportunities (ES-11, ES-20, ES-06, ES-08, ES-16) align with the highest-scoring feature in Step 09 — **Digital Anomaly Detection (fit=7.56)** — which directly serves empty-pipe, incipient-leak, and loss-of-flow detection. **No Moving Parts (fit=6.78)** supports ES-09/ES-16. Biggest mismatch is ES-22 (replace without draining, opp=13) — FIT §11 flags Marquardt's plug-in mechanical interface as a vendor advantage but serial production is DN20-only. Communication-protocol gap (FIT §11a must_be=3) does not appear in ODI top-5 because Mag incumbents already satisfy ES-05 at sat=7.

---

sections.title: QA Checklist
sections.content:
| # | Check | Result | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 | Formula correct | **PASS** | Spot-check: ES-11 (10+max(10-5,0)=15), ES-20 (9+max(9-3,0)=15), ES-13 (7+max(7-7,0)=7), PRN-02 (5+max(5-7,0)=5), ES-22 (8+max(8-3,0)=13). All match. |
| Q2 | Underserved flags correct | **PASS** | 5 entries with imp>7 AND sat<5: ES-06(9,4), ES-08(9,4), ES-12(8,3), ES-20(9,3), ES-22(8,3). ES-11(10,5)=NO (5 not <5). |
| Q3 | Overserved flags correct | **PASS** | 0 entries with imp<3 AND sat>7. Minimum importance across all 34 entries is 5. |
| Q4 | Need count preserved | **PASS** | 26 (Step 06) + 8 (Step 10) = 34 ≥ 26 |
| Q5 | Top-5 ordering correct | **PASS** | 15 ≥ 15 ≥ 14 ≥ 14 ≥ 13 — descending by opp; ties broken by importance then need# |
| Q6 | Cross-check ran | **PASS** | ODI vs Feature-Fit comparison completed; no inconsistencies |

---

rows.Strengths: ±0.5% accuracy, no moving parts, DN2–DN150, HART/Modbus/PROFIBUS/EtherNet-IP, 100:1 turndown, bidirectional, empty-pipe detection via conductivity
rows.Weaknesses: 5–10× cost, larger footprint, higher power, flanged not plug-in, requires conductive fluid
Bearing wear, pressure drop, no diagnostics, ferrous particle jamming
Min-Reynolds floor, vibration sensitive, poor at low flow
On/off only, no kW duty, no telemetry

rows.Rationale: Mag 100:1 turndown covers variable-speed; turbine 30:1 adequate
Mag DN2–DN150 with common transmitter; Promag H full coverage
Mag needs no fluid config; ultrasonic/vortex do. Mixed
Mag orientation-insensitive; ultrasonic/vortex biased
Mag ships HART/Modbus/PROFIBUS/EtherNet-IP native
Mag has conductivity-based empty-pipe; turbine/vortex cannot
Mag via HART; turbine none
Flow reading available; OCP-specific interlock logic not provided
Mag drift-stable; turbine drifts 2–5%; mixed
Mag insensitive to air; turbine jams; ultrasonic loses signal
Mag response 0.1–1s adequate; turbine slower
Integrated temp is rare across incumbents; Promag H optional
Mag zero-drop; turbine/vortex moderate drop
Mag has damping; ultrasonic/turbine susceptible
Mag bidirectional; turbine/paddle cannot resolve direction
Mag stable but no degradation trending; turbine drifts silently
Mag stable but no continuous drift alarm logic
Mag with Modbus/EtherNet-IP streams at 1 Hz
Mag electrodes tolerate fouling; turbine blades accumulate
No incumbent provides sub-threshold leak pattern recognition
Ultrasonic-specific; mag unaffected but minority share
Mag flanged/bulky; turbine/vortex inline — all require drain
Mag rarely needs field recal; turbine wears and drifts
Mag HART limited OTA; no incumbent offers full remote update
Mag with ISO 17025 traceability available; turbine rare
Mag reliability good but cost-driven underdeployment
Mag insensitive to sound speed; ultrasonic-only failure mode
Metal-housed mag/Coriolis rated 130 °C+

rows.Statement (abbreviated): DN20 4.2 L/min SNR floor vs OCP
rows.Rationale: Mag resolves sub-0.1 m/s; ultrasonic hits SNR floor
Mag insensitive; turbine jams; mixed incumbent coverage
Mag conductivity empty-pipe flag; mechanical impeller still rotates in partial fill

rows.Statement (abbreviated): Single-chord path bias in compact manifold
rows.Rationale: Multi-path ultrasonic/mag are profile-insensitive
Mag temp-independent by principle
Metal-bodied mag/Coriolis rated 16–40 bar

entities.market_name: Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services

entries.error_statement: Minimize the likelihood of selecting a flow sensor whose turndown ratio cannot cover variable-speed pump operation from idle to peak GPU load
Increase the ability to specify one sensor platform across DN15-DN25 CDU variants without redesigning the secondary manifold
Minimize the commissioning time required to configure fluid acoustic properties for facility-specific water / PG25 / EG25 blends
Minimize the likelihood of incorrect installation orientation causing flow measurement bias exceeding 2% of reading
Increase the ability to integrate the sensor into existing CDU BMS stacks (Modbus / BACnet / MQTT) without custom gateway development
Minimize the time to detect empty-pipe or air-lock in the secondary loop before IT power-on to under 5 seconds
Increase the ability to verify sensor signal integrity through automated self-diagnostics at each CDU start cycle
Minimize the likelihood of enabling GPU load with secondary-loop flow below the OCP 1.5 L/min/kW safety threshold
Increase the ability to maintain flow accuracy within +/-2% of reading over 40,000 hours of continuous 24x7 operation
Minimize the likelihood of air entrainment >2% vol after glycol top-up causing signal loss during a GPU workload ramp
Minimize the time to detect a step-change loss-of-flow event (leak, valve closure, pump trip) to under 2 seconds
Increase the ability to compute secondary-loop thermal duty (kW) from flow x delta-T within a single sensor body, sub-second
Minimize the flow sensor's contribution to CDU secondary-loop pressure drop below 50 mbar at 30 L/min design flow
Minimize the likelihood of pump-pulsation-induced flow reading oscillation exceeding 5% of steady-state value
Increase the ability to detect reverse-flow direction (bypass-valve misoperation) within 1 second of onset
Minimize the likelihood of GPU throttling events triggered by undetected flow degradation exceeding 10% from setpoint
Increase the ability to detect calibration drift exceeding +/-0.5% within 30 days of occurrence over 24 months continuous operation
Increase the ability to stream 1-second-granularity flow + temperature telemetry into DCIM for real-time PUE/WUE reporting
Minimize the likelihood of biofilm / scale / particulate buildup degrading accuracy by more than 2% within 5-year service life
Increase the ability to detect incipient leak signatures (sub-threshold flow decay) before visible coolant loss
Minimize the likelihood of acoustic-coupling degradation causing error >1% within the first 5 years of operation
Minimize the time required to replace a degraded flow sensor without draining the secondary loop or taking the CDU offline
Minimize the frequency of mandated field recalibration to no more than once per 5-year CDU service cycle
Increase the ability to push firmware / compensation-table updates to the sensor remotely via the CDU BMS bus
Increase the ability to evidence ASHRAE TC 9.9 H-class liquid-cooling envelope compliance through traceable per-rack flow telemetry
Minimize the frequency of hyperscaler customer escalations attributed to flow-sensor malfunction within first 24 months after CDU deployment

Minimize the likelihood of measurement drift from acoustic-impedance mismatch when facility-specific glycol inhibitor packages alter the coolant's speed of sound beyond the commissioned compensation profile
Minimize the residual headroom concern when future ASHRAE W4/W5 warm-water liquid cooling drives CDU secondary-loop supply to the 90 C polymer housing limit
Minimize the unmeasured-flow window at the DN20 4.2 L/min SNR threshold during CDU idle / low-load states, below which the OCP 1.5 L/min/kW interlock cannot discriminate low flow from no flow

Minimize the probability of sub-threshold air entrainment (1-2% vol) following glycol top-up or venting biasing flow telemetry fed to the OCP safety interlock, without triggering the >2% loss-of-signal alarm

Minimize the time the CDU controller spends in empty-pipe fault state during secondary-loop drain-and-fill maintenance cycles, during which the sensor provides no measurement and the prove-flow gate cannot be satisfied

Minimize the installation-induced flow-profile bias from insufficient upstream straight-pipe run (<5x DN) inside space-constrained rack CDU manifolds where single-chord acoustic path sampling cannot correct for swirl

Minimize the transient kW duty miscalculation when a GPU workload spike drives secondary-supply temperature transients >5 C/min that outpace the NTC thermistor's compensation response, during which flow x delta-T telemetry deviates from true thermal load

Minimize the deployment gap at hyperscaler facility-loop pressures exceeding 10 bar where the polymer housing rating forecloses direct-facility-loop installation and forces placement downstream of the brazed-plate HX
